title: Roasted Butternut Squash Salad with Tahini and Za'atar
intro: Sweet caramelized squash meets a nutty tahini drizzle and a bright scatter of herbs. This warm salad is generous, layered, and unmistakably Middle Eastern in spirit.
ingredients: 1 large butternut squash, peeled and cut into 2 cm wedges
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Heat the oven to 220C and toss the squash and onion with 2 tablespoons olive oil, salt, and pepper on a large tray.
Roast for 30 to 35 minutes until the edges are deeply caramelized and the flesh is tender.
Whisk together the tahini, garlic, lemon juice, water, and a pinch of salt until it forms a smooth pourable sauce.
Loosen the sauce with a little more water if needed so it drizzles easily.
Arrange the warm roasted vegetables across a serving platter in a loose single layer.
Spoon the tahini sauce generously over the top and scatter with toasted pine nuts.
Finish with a shower of za'atar and fresh cilantro leaves before serving warm.
tip: Roast the squash until the edges are almost burnt; that deep color is where the sweetness lives.

title: Barefoot Green Salad with Champagne Vinaigrette
intro: A crisp bowl of tender greens dressed in a bright, mustardy champagne vinaigrette. Simple, elegant, and the kind of salad that quietly steals the meal.
ingredients: 8 cups mixed baby greens and butter lettuce
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Whisk the champagne vinegar, Dijon mustard, minced shallot, honey, salt, and pepper together in a small bowl.
Slowly stream in the olive oil while whisking until the dressing emulsifies and thickens slightly.
Wash and thoroughly dry the greens so the dressing clings rather than slides.
Place the greens in a large wide bowl just before serving.
Drizzle over just enough vinaigrette to lightly coat and toss gently with your hands.
Shave Parmesan over the top and add a final grind of black pepper before serving.
tip: Dress the greens only moments before serving so they stay lively and never wilt.

title: Jamie's Crunchy Rainbow Slaw with Lemon and Mint
intro: A riot of raw color tossed in a zippy lemon dressing with a lift of fresh mint. This is a fast, no-cook side that brings real crunch to any plate.
ingredients: 1 crisp apple, cut into thin matchsticks
1 small handful fresh mint leaves, chopped
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Combine the shredded cabbage, grated carrot, grated beetroot, and apple matchsticks in a large bowl.
Whisk the olive oil, lemon juice, honey, salt, and pepper together in a small jug.
Pour the dressing over the vegetables and toss well until everything glistens.
Let the slaw sit for five minutes so the flavors mingle and the cabbage softens slightly.
Fold through most of the chopped mint just before serving.
Scatter the remaining mint and toasted sunflower seeds over the top for crunch.
tip: Grate the beetroot last and toss quickly to keep its color from bleeding across the whole bowl.

title: Nigella's Warm Halloumi and Watermelon Salad
intro: Salty pan-fried halloumi against cool sweet watermelon is a study in glorious contrast. A handful of mint and a squeeze of lime pull it all together.
ingredients: 250 g halloumi, sliced into 1 cm slabs
600 g watermelon, cut into bite-sized cubes
2 tablespoons black olives, pitted and halved
steps: Pat the halloumi slices dry with paper towels so they sear rather than steam.
Heat the olive oil in a nonstick pan over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Fry the halloumi for two to three minutes per side until deeply golden and crisp.
Arrange the watermelon cubes across a serving platter and scatter over the olives.
Lay the hot halloumi over the watermelon while it is still sizzling.
Squeeze the lime juice over everything and grind on plenty of black pepper.
Finish with the sliced chili and torn mint leaves and serve immediately.
tip: Serve the moment the halloumi comes off the heat; it turns rubbery once it cools.

title: Chez Panisse Garden Lettuces with Baked Goat Cheese
intro: Warm, herb-crusted rounds of goat cheese rest on a bed of the tenderest garden lettuces. It is a quiet celebration of simple ingredients treated with real care.
ingredients: 150 g fresh goat cheese log, chilled
2 tablespoons olive oil for the cheese
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Slice the chilled goat cheese into four rounds and brush each with olive oil.
Mix the breadcrumbs with the thyme, then press the rounds firmly into the crumbs to coat.
Chill the coated rounds for ten minutes while you heat the oven to 200C.
Bake the cheese rounds on a lined tray for about eight minutes until golden and just softened.
Whisk the red wine vinegar, Dijon, salt, and pepper, then stream in the dressing olive oil.
Toss the lettuces lightly with just enough vinaigrette to coat each leaf.
Divide the greens among plates and set a warm goat cheese round on each before serving.
tip: Keep the cheese cold right up until baking so it holds its shape and does not ooze away.

title: Giada's Italian Chopped Salad with Salami and Provolone
intro: Every forkful of this hearty chopped salad delivers salami, cheese, and crisp greens in one bite. A punchy oregano vinaigrette makes it taste like a great Italian deli.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Whisk the red wine vinegar, dried oregano, salt, and pepper in a large salad bowl.
Stream in the olive oil while whisking until the vinaigrette comes together.
Add the chopped romaine and radicchio directly on top of the dressing.
Pile in the diced salami, provolone, tomatoes, chickpeas, and pepperoncini.
Toss everything thoroughly so every piece is evenly coated and mingled.
Taste and adjust the salt, then serve right away while the greens are crisp.
tip: Dice all the add-ins to roughly the same size so each forkful is perfectly balanced.

title: Gordon's Seared Beef and Rocket Salad with Parmesan
intro: Rare seared steak fanned over peppery rocket makes a restaurant-worthy main-course salad. Sharp Parmesan and a mustard dressing give it real backbone.
ingredients: 400 g sirloin steak, at room temperature
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
steps: Season the steak generously all over with salt and freshly ground pepper.
Heat the searing oil in a heavy pan until almost smoking, then sear the steak for two to three minutes per side for medium-rare.
Transfer the steak to a board and let it rest for at least five minutes.
Whisk the balsamic vinegar, Dijon, and extra virgin olive oil into a glossy dressing.
Toss the rocket and cherry tomatoes with just enough dressing to coat lightly.
Slice the rested steak thinly against the grain into elegant strips.
Fan the beef over the dressed leaves and finish with shaved Parmesan and a grind of pepper.
tip: Rest the steak fully before slicing so the juices stay in the meat instead of on the board.

title: Jose's Spanish Tomato Salad with Sherry Vinegar and Almonds
intro: Ripe summer tomatoes shine with nothing more than great olive oil, sherry vinegar, and crunchy Marcona almonds. This is Spanish simplicity at its most confident.
ingredients: 700 g mixed ripe tomatoes, cut into thick wedges
4 tablespoons Spanish extra virgin olive oil
1 small garlic clove, very finely grated
steps: Arrange the tomato wedges in a single layer across a wide serving plate.
Season them generously with flaky sea salt and let them sit for five minutes to draw out their juices.
Whisk the sherry vinegar, grated garlic, and olive oil into a bright dressing.
Spoon the dressing evenly over the salted tomatoes.
Scatter the chopped Marcona almonds across the top for crunch.
Finish with torn basil leaves and a final crack of black pepper before serving.
tip: Use tomatoes at full room temperature; refrigeration mutes their flavor and aroma.

title: Nicoise Salad with Seared Tuna and Green Beans
intro: This composed Provencal classic layers seared tuna, waxy potatoes, and crisp beans over bright leaves. Arranged with care, it is a complete and beautiful main-course salad.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Boil the potatoes until tender, then drain and halve them while still warm.
Cook the green beans in salted boiling water for three minutes, then plunge into ice water to keep them crisp.
Boil the eggs for eight minutes, cool them, then peel and quarter.
Season the tuna and sear in a hot oiled pan for one to two minutes per side, leaving the center pink, then slice.
Whisk the red wine vinegar, Dijon, salt, and pepper, then stream in the olive oil to make the dressing.
Line a platter with lettuce and arrange the potatoes, beans, tomatoes, eggs, and olives in separate groupings.
Lay the sliced tuna in the center and spoon the vinaigrette over the whole platter before serving.
tip: Arrange each element in its own section rather than tossing, so the salad stays clean and striking.

title: Kale Caesar with Garlic Anchovy Dressing and Crisp Crumbs
intro: Sturdy kale stands up to a bold, briny Caesar dressing built on real anchovies and garlic. Toasted sourdough crumbs replace croutons for an addictive savory crunch.
ingredients: 1 large bunch curly kale, stripped and torn
2 tablespoons olive oil for the crumbs
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Toast the sourdough crumbs in the searing oil over medium heat until deeply golden, then season with salt and set aside.
Mash the anchovies and garlic into a smooth paste with the side of a knife.
Whisk the anchovy paste with the egg yolk, lemon juice, and Dijon in a large bowl.
Slowly stream in the olive oil while whisking until the dressing turns thick and creamy.
Stir most of the grated Parmesan into the dressing and season with pepper.
Add the torn kale and massage the dressing into the leaves for a minute to soften them.
Scatter over the crisp crumbs and remaining Parmesan just before serving.
tip: Massaging the dressing into the kale is essential; it tenderizes the leaves and tames their bitterness.

title: Thai Green Papaya Salad with Lime and Peanuts
intro: Shredded green papaya is pounded with chili, lime, and fish sauce for an explosive sweet-sour-salty hit. Crunchy peanuts and beans make it fresh, fiery, and unforgettable.
ingredients: 1 small green papaya, peeled and shredded
6 green beans, cut into short lengths
steps: Pound the garlic and chilies in a large mortar until they break down into a rough paste.
Add the green beans and lightly bruise them so they absorb the dressing.
Spoon in the palm sugar, fish sauce, and lime juice, then pound gently to dissolve the sugar.
Add the shredded papaya and tomatoes, then pound and turn with a spoon to bruise without crushing.
Fold in the dried shrimp if using and taste for a balance of sour, salty, sweet, and hot.
Tip the salad onto a plate and scatter over the roasted peanuts before serving.
tip: Bruise rather than pulverize the papaya so it stays crunchy while soaking up the dressing.

title: Fattoush with Sumac, Crisp Pita and Pomegranate
intro: Shards of crisp toasted pita catch a tangy sumac and pomegranate dressing in this Levantine favorite. It is herby, crunchy, and bursting with fresh vegetables.
ingredients: 3 tablespoons olive oil for the pita
Salt and black pepper to taste
steps: Toss the torn pita with the searing olive oil and a pinch of salt, then toast at 200C for about eight minutes until crisp.
Whisk the sumac, lemon juice, pomegranate molasses, and dressing olive oil into a tangy dressing.
Combine the cucumbers, tomatoes, radishes, and spring onions in a large bowl.
Add the chopped parsley and mint to the vegetables.
Pour over the dressing and toss so everything is evenly coated.
Fold in the crisp pita shards just before serving so they keep their crunch.
Finish with an extra dusting of sumac over the top.
tip: Add the toasted pita at the very last moment so it stays crackly instead of turning soggy.
